intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gentle, approachable interfaces for health tracking over clinical-looking sharp designs
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use curved card borders, button radii, and smooth element transitions to feel less medical/sterile
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard can tolerate sharper, more structured geometry; clinical precision may feel appropriate for health data

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer gentle, approachable interfaces for health tracking over clinical-looking sharp designs
evidence.implication: Dashboard should use curved card borders, button radii, and smooth element transitions to feel less medical/sterile

facade.hypothesis: Users building personal health dashboards respond better to gentle, approachable UI than sharp or minimal interfaces
facade.label: Soft rounded cards
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace organic, touchable aesthetics with curves and generous padding throughout the dashboard
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward cleaner, more geometric or minimalist visual language with tighter spacing

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a conversational, low-pressure tone for daily logging over clinical/medical language
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality: friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Design uses warm, conversational language ('How's your energy today?' instead of 'Log vitals'). Friendly emoji or illustrations. Approachable onboarding.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want professional, clinical tone. Use formal language ('Patient log', 'Vital metrics'). Minimal personality. Data-first presentation.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gradual animated transitions for metric reveals over instant data dumps, reducing cognitive load and building narrative momentum in health tracking.
facade.label: Smooth slide-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into motion: stagger metric loads, use easing curves on stat reveals, let progress bars animate naturally. Motion signals progression in health data.
facade.rejectImplies: Embrace instant: all data loads at once, no transition delays, snappy and efficient. Users scan, not follow. Motion is friction.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer highlighting one key metric per health experiment (e.g., '42 days') over dense tables or multi-metric cards
facade.axisTargeted: granularity—single metric prominence vs. multi-data display
facade.label: Big number, small context
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should prioritize hero metrics; minimize surrounding data; use whitespace; cards show one number + one supporting detail only
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should pack information density; use tables or row-based layouts; show multiple metrics per experiment simultaneously; prioritize data completeness over visual hierarchy

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer dark interfaces for evening health tracking to reduce eye strain and feel less clinical than bright white dashboards
facade.label: Dark mode
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should offer dark theme option with soft contrasts; nighttime tracking sessions are important to the experience
facade.rejectImplies: Users want bright, clean, medical-style interfaces; dark mode feels unprofessional or harder to read for health data

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar for experiment categories paired with card-based layouts for individual experiments creates better scanability and mental organization than a full-page tabbed interface.
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent context and visual separation; likely prefers hierarchical navigation; probably wants quick category switching without losing place.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer flat, tab-based navigation; might find sidebars claustrophobic; possibly values maximum content space over persistent navigation markers.

draft.title: Health Experiment Tracker
draft.summary: A personal dashboard for monitoring long-term health experiments with experiment cards, metrics, and progress visualization.
draft.html:
Health Experiments

Track your long-term health initiatives

Active Experiments

Sleep Optimization

Active

Testing consistent 10 PM bedtime with blue light reduction

Day 26 of 60 65%
Avg Sleep Duration 7.2 hrs
Sleep Quality 7.8/10

Intermittent Fasting

Active

16:8 fasting window with energy and digestion tracking

Day 17 of 40 42%
Adherence Rate 94%
Energy Level 6.5/10
Completed Experiments

Morning Hydration

Completed

Drinking 500ml water within 30 min of waking

Day 30 of 30 100%

draft.nextHint: User accepted soft rounded corners, signaling preference for warm, approachable design. Next probes should explore: (1) data density vs. progressive disclosure, (2) motivational tone vs. neutral reporting, (3) celebratory feedback vs. calm tracking. Consider testing a variant with encouraging language or one with more minimal data display to discover their preference on these unresolved axes.
